description_long: This object is a rectangular stone obelisk with a broad, flat top and a sturdy, tapering shaft that sits on a square plinth. The surface is marbled with reddish and brownish hues, giving it a weathered, aged appearance typical of monuments found in parks or urban plazas. The base is thick and slightly stepped, with a beveled edge that transitions into the main shaft. The obelisk is set on a patch of earth or sand, suggesting it is installed outdoors, possibly in a landscaped or historical area. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative carvings, and the overall design is simple and robust. The stone appears solid and heavy, with a rough, matte finish.
description: A rectangular, reddish-brown stone obelisk with a broad, flat top and a thick, stepped base, set on a patch of earth. The surface is marbled and weathered, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad face of the obelisk, highlighting the marbled stone texture and the thick, stepped base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the base and the rectangular shaft, with more visible weathering on the stone.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back face, similar in design to the front, with subtle color variations and a slightly uneven earth patch.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the other profile of the obelisk, emphasizing the symmetry and the robust, solid construction.
attribute_base_design: The base is a thick, square plinth with a stepped, beveled edge, constructed from the same marbled stone as the shaft. It is robust and slightly raised above the surrounding earth.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and broad, with no pointed or decorative features, maintaining the rectangular profile of the shaft.
attribute_surrounding_features: The obelisk is surrounded by a patch of earth or sand, with no other notable features or objects immediately adjacent.
